transcript.text: The runtime picture is getting clear: the real app loads `index.css`, wraps everything in `LikedArticlesProvider`, reads `localStorage` for `likedArticles` and `lang`, fetches Wikipedia data through `useWikiArticles`, preloads images with `new Image()`, and uses plain fixed overlays rather than React portals. Next I’m checking the Storybook/Vitest setup and package manager details so the shared preview can slot into the existing toolchain cleanly.

transcript.text: I’ve now mapped the real behavior we need to preserve: `StrictMode` + `LikedArticlesProvider`, `index.css`, `localStorage`-backed likes and language, Wikipedia fetches, and fixed overlay panels inside `App` rather than portal roots. I’m checking what’s already in Storybook and then I’ll replace the starter setup with one shared environment, add stories around the real components, and keep the mocks global so the stories stay thin.

transcript.text: I have enough context and I’m editing the shared setup now. The changes are focused in `.storybook` plus new colocated stories, and I’m keeping the stories thin by pushing CSS, provider wiring, localStorage seeding, and Wikipedia mocks into the preview layer.

The shared preview and the first pass of the new stories are in place. I’m in verification mode now, and I expect a couple of iteration rounds to tighten selectors and typings because the app is running under the real provider/runtime behavior rather than a simplified harness.

transcript.text: TypeScript is clean, so I’m tightening the story assertions before browser tests. The main thing now is making the `play` functions resilient to the app’s real full-page rendering and `StrictMode` behavior, especially where repeated headings or unlabeled inputs can make selectors flaky.

The browser run is in progress now. I’m watching for the first failing interaction, and if anything breaks it’ll most likely be from real runtime behavior like duplicate renders, async image/load timing, or DOM selection around the fixed overlays.

transcript.text: I’ve got the failures narrowed down now. The shared mocks are too faithful in one spot and are replaying identical feed responses under `StrictMode`, and a couple of assertions need to scope themselves to the likes panel instead of the whole page. I’m fixing those, and I’m also making the component stories use a visibly in-flow shell so Storybook’s empty-render guard sees the same UI we do.

The second browser pass is running with the stricter mock reset and scoped modal assertions. At this point I’m mostly looking for any remaining empty-canvas false positives or event-target quirks, especially in the component-only stories.

The request loop turned out to be caused by the “empty after first fetch” strategy rather than the selectors. I’m switching the Wikipedia mock to stay deterministic but always return real cards with unique page IDs, which should keep the feed stable under repeated fetches without changing the app code.

Storybook now mirrors the real app’s runtime instead of the starter scaffold. The shared setup in [preview.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-06-29Z-953f5a4d/project/frontend/.storybook/preview.tsx) imports the real [index.css](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-06-29Z-953f5a4d/project/frontend/src/index.css), wraps stories with the real [LikedArticlesProvider](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-06-29Z-953f5a4d/project/frontend/src/contexts/LikedArticlesContext.tsx), seeds the actual browser state the app reads (`lang`, `likedArticles`), and wires global MSW handlers from [msw-handlers.ts](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-06-29Z-953f5a4d/project/frontend/.storybook/msw-handlers.ts). I also updated [main.ts](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-06-29Z-953f5a4d/project/frontend/.storybook/main.ts) to serve `public` and only load the real app stories, added the mock worker at [mockServiceWorker.js](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-06-29Z-953f5a4d/project/frontend/public/mockServiceWorker.js), and added the MSW addon dependency in [package.json](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-06-29Z-953f5a4d/project/frontend/package.json). I didn’t add `preview-body.html` because the app doesn’t use React portals; its overlays are fixed-position elements inside `App`.

I replaced the demo stories with real, colocated stories in [App.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-06-29Z-953f5a4d/project/frontend/src/App.stories.tsx), [WikiCard.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-06-29Z-953f5a4d/project/frontend/src/components/WikiCard.stories.tsx), and [LanguageSelector.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-06-29Z-953f5a4d/project/frontend/src/components/LanguageSelector.stories.tsx). Every named export has a `play` function, and those plays verify real behavior: fetched Wikipedia cards render, loading states resolve, About/Likes overlays open, likes persist through the provider-backed UI, search filters the liked list, the card like interaction updates state, and the language dropdown opens/closes.

Verification is clean:
```bash
npx tsc --noEmit
STORYBOOK_FAIL_ON_EMPTY_RENDER=true npx vitest --project storybook src/App.stories.tsx src/components/WikiCard.stories.tsx src/components/LanguageSelector.stories.tsx
```

One note: there was already an unrelated untracked file at `.storybook/eval-results/prompt.md`; I left that alone.

Next I’m checking the Storybook/Vitest setup and package manager details so the shared preview can slot into the existing toolchain cleanly.

content.text: TypeScript is clean, so I’m tightening the story assertions before browser tests. The main thing now is making the `play` functions resilient to the app’s real full-page rendering and `StrictMode` behavior, especially where repeated headings or unlabeled inputs can make selectors flaky.

File changes:
- update /Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-06-29Z-953f5a4d/project/frontend/src/App.stories.tsx

The browser run is in progress now. I’m watching for the first failing interaction, and if anything breaks it’ll most likely be from real runtime behavior like duplicate renders, async image/load timing, or DOM selection around the fixed overlays.

content.text: I’ve got the failures narrowed down now. The shared mocks are too faithful in one spot and are replaying identical feed responses under `StrictMode`, and a couple of assertions need to scope themselves to the likes panel instead of the whole page. I’m fixing those, and I’m also making the component stories use a visibly in-flow shell so Storybook’s empty-render guard sees the same UI we do.

content.text: The second browser pass is running with the stricter mock reset and scoped modal assertions. At this point I’m mostly looking for any remaining empty-canvas false positives or event-target quirks, especially in the component-only stories.

The request loop turned out to be caused by the “empty after first fetch” strategy rather than the selectors. I’m switching the Wikipedia mock to stay deterministic but always return real cards with unique page IDs, which should keep the feed stable under repeated fetches without changing the app code.

content.text:
Storybook now mirrors the real app’s runtime instead of the starter scaffold. The shared setup in [preview.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-06-29Z-953f5a4d/project/frontend/.storybook/preview.tsx) imports the real [index.css](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-06-29Z-953f5a4d/project/frontend/src/index.css), wraps stories with the real [LikedArticlesProvider](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-06-29Z-953f5a4d/project/frontend/src/contexts/LikedArticlesContext.tsx), seeds the actual browser state the app reads (`lang`, `likedArticles`), and wires global MSW handlers from [msw-handlers.ts](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-06-29Z-953f5a4d/project/frontend/.storybook/msw-handlers.ts). I also updated [main.ts](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-06-29Z-953f5a4d/project/frontend/.storybook/main.ts) to serve `public` and only load the real app stories, added the mock worker at [mockServiceWorker.js](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-06-29Z-953f5a4d/project/frontend/public/mockServiceWorker.js), and added the MSW addon dependency in [package.json](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-06-29Z-953f5a4d/project/frontend/package.json). I didn’t add `preview-body.html` because the app doesn’t use React portals; its overlays are fixed-position elements inside `App`.

I replaced the demo stories with real, colocated stories in [App.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-06-29Z-953f5a4d/project/frontend/src/App.stories.tsx), [WikiCard.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-06-29Z-953f5a4d/project/frontend/src/components/WikiCard.stories.tsx), and [LanguageSelector.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-06-29Z-953f5a4d/project/frontend/src/components/LanguageSelector.stories.tsx). Every named export has a `play` function, and those plays verify real behavior: fetched Wikipedia cards render, loading states resolve, About/Likes overlays open, likes persist through the provider-backed UI, search filters the liked list, the card like interaction updates state, and the language dropdown opens/closes.

Verification is clean:
```bash
npx tsc --noEmit
STORYBOOK_FAIL_ON_EMPTY_RENDER=true npx vitest --project storybook src/App.stories.tsx src/components/WikiCard.stories.tsx src/components/LanguageSelector.stories.tsx
```

One note: there was already an unrelated untracked file at `.storybook/eval-results/prompt.md`; I left that alone.
